criteria.description: sum.js exists at the project root and uses ESM (consistent with package.json "type":"module"). It exports a function via a named export `sum` (export function sum / export const sum). No CommonJS module.exports/require. Import must not produce console output or other side effects.
criteria.verifyBy: Inspect sum.js: confirm it contains an ESM `export` of `sum` and no `module.exports`/`require`. Run `node -e "import('./sum.js').then(m=>{if(typeof m.sum!=='function')process.exit(1)})"` and confirm exit code 0 with no stdout on import.

criteria.description: The implementation body of sum is exactly the addition of its two parameters (return a + b) with no branching, conditionals, or lookup tables. This guards against example-hardcoding and keeps it faithful to the goal sum(a,b)=a+b.
criteria.verifyBy: Source inspection of sum.js: the function body returns `a + b` (or the two named params summed with +), containing no if/switch/ternary/array/object lookup keyed on argument values.

criteria.description: sum(a,b) returns a+b for positive integers using the exported symbol from ./sum.js: sum(1,2)===3.
criteria.verifyBy: Run `node --test sum.test.js`; a test using `import { sum } from './sum.js'` asserts assert.strictEqual(sum(1,2),3) and passes.

criteria.description: sum handles negative numbers via the imported symbol: sum(-4,-6)===-10 and sum(-5,3)===-2.
criteria.verifyBy: Run `node --test sum.test.js`; tests assert assert.strictEqual(sum(-4,-6),-10) and assert.strictEqual(sum(-5,3),-2) pass, exercising the import from ./sum.js.

criteria.description: sum handles zero via the imported symbol: sum(0,0)===0 and sum(0,7)===7.
criteria.verifyBy: Run `node --test sum.test.js`; tests assert assert.strictEqual(sum(0,0),0) and assert.strictEqual(sum(0,7),7) pass.

criteria.description: sum handles floating-point correctly: (a) an exactly-representable case verified with strict equality assert.strictEqual(sum(0.5,0.25),0.75); AND (b) the sum(0.1,0.2) case verified only with an epsilon comparison assert.ok(Math.abs(sum(0.1,0.2)-0.3)<1e-9). The test MUST NOT use assert.strictEqual(sum(0.1,0.2),0.3).
criteria.verifyBy: Run `node --test sum.test.js`; confirm both the strict-equal 0.75 case and the epsilon 0.1+0.2 case pass. Inspect test file to confirm no assert.strictEqual comparing sum(0.1,0.2) to 0.3.

criteria.description: A randomized property check verifies faithfulness: for at least 100 random (a,b) pairs, sum(a,b)===a+b using the imported symbol.
criteria.verifyBy: Run `node --test sum.test.js`; a test loops over >=100 random numeric pairs and asserts assert.strictEqual(sum(a,b), a+b) for each, all passing.

criteria.description: The test file is named exactly sum.test.js at the project root, uses ESM, imports node:test and node:assert (import test from 'node:test'; import assert from 'node:assert'/'node:assert/strict'), imports { sum } from './sum.js', and contains all the above cases. All assertions exercise the symbol imported from ./sum.js (no locally redefined sum).
criteria.verifyBy: Inspect sum.test.js: confirm ESM imports of node:test, node:assert, and { sum } from './sum.js'; confirm no inline redefinition of sum; confirm the c3-c7 cases are present.

criteria.description: All tests run and pass via `node --test sum.test.js` (scoped to this file, not a global run), with zero failing subtests.
criteria.verifyBy: Execute `node --test sum.test.js`; confirm exit code 0 and the TAP summary reports `# fail 0` and `# pass` >= 6 (covering positive, negative, zero, exact-float, epsilon-float, and property cases).
